steps.title: Use the exterior angle
steps.content:
When side $NP$ is extended through $P$, angle $MPT$ is an exterior angle of triangle $MNP$. An exterior angle equals the sum of the two remote interior angles:
$$
x+y=(3x+4)+(2x+9)
$$
$$
y=4x+13
$$

steps.title: Use the triangle sum
steps.content:
The three interior angles of triangle $MNP$ sum to $180^\circ$:
$$
(3x+4)+(2x+9)+(4y+10)=180
$$
$$
5x+4y=157
$$

steps.title: Solve for the exterior angle
steps.content: Substitute $y=4x+13$: $$ 5x+4(4x+13)=157 $$ $$ 21x=105 $$ $$ x=5 $$ Then $y=4(5)+13=33$, so $x+y=38$. The answer is 38.
